title: humboltia laurifolia
intro: Humboldtia laurifolia, also known as the Malabar nut tree, is a tropical plant native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant's seeds are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and they contain a unique alkaloid called obacunone, which has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Humboldtia laurifolia include its potential to reduce fever, treat respiratory infections, and provide antioxidant protection. In traditional medicine, the seeds are often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to create a herbal tea, and they can also be powdered and taken as a supplement.
